diagnosis:
1) compare-two-configs: read_text_file's description explicitly says "Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file," but it doesn't warn the agent away from it when multiple files are involved, while read_multiple_files' description says it's "more efficient" (a soft nice-to-have, not a directive) rather than "the required/preferred tool for 2+ files." The agent defaulted to the tool it already used for single-file cases and just called it twice, since nothing in read_text_file's text says "don't use this for multiple files, use read_multiple_files instead."

2) create-new-file: write_file's description doesn't state that it will create any missing parent directories automatically. Because create_directory's description says it's "Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist," the agent inferred it should preemptively call create_directory before write_file just in case the parent folder doesn't exist, adding an unnecessary call in ~40% of runs.

3) make-nested-directory: create_directory's description doesn't explicitly state that it creates all necessary parent/intermediate directories in a single call and is idempotent/safe to call once. The phrase "Can create multiple nested directories in one operation" is ambiguous — it can be read as "can create several separate directory trees in one call" rather than "creates all missing intermediate segments of a single nested path automatically." This ambiguity, plus no explicit statement that repeated/verifying calls are unnecessary, caused the agent to over-verify: it called create_directory multiple times, checked list_allowed_directories and list_directory, and manually created parent segments before the final target, none of which was needed.
</diagnosis>
<rewrites>
<tool>read_text_file</tool>
<new_description>Read the complete contents of a single text file from the file system. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use the 'head' parameter to read only the first N lines, or 'tail' to read only the last N lines. Operates on the file as text regardless of extension. For comparing or examining two or more files at once, use read_multiple_files instead of calling this tool repeatedly. Only works within allowed directories.</new_description>
<rationale>Explicitly redirects multi-file requests to read_multiple_files instead of letting the agent default to repeated single-file reads.</rationale>
</tool>
<tool>read_multiple_files</tool>
<new_description>Preferred tool whenever you need to read, examine, or compare two or more files: reads all of their contents in a single call. Each file's content is returned labeled with its path, and a failed read for one file will not stop the others from being read. Always use this instead of calling read_text_file multiple times when more than one file is involved. Only works within allowed directories.</new_description>
<rationale>Makes it the mandatory choice for any multi-file task, not just an "efficiency" suggestion, so the agent picks it over repeated single reads.</rationale>
</tool>
<tool>write_file</tool>
<new_description>Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with the given text content. Automatically creates the file at the exact path given; there is no need to call create_directory first if the parent directory already exists. Use with caution as it overwrites existing files without warning. Only works within allowed directories.</new_description>
<rationale>Removes ambiguity that led the agent to preemptively call create_directory before writing a file in a directory that already exists.</rationale>
</tool>
<tool>create_directory</tool>
<new_description>Create a directory at the given path in a single call, automatically creating any missing intermediate parent directories (like 'mkdir -p'). Idempotent: if the directory (or any parent) already exists, the call still succeeds silently with no error — so only one call is needed, and there is no need to verify existence beforehand or create parent segments separately. Only works within allowed directories.</new_description>
<rationale>Clarifies that one call handles the entire nested path and is safe/idempotent, eliminating the repeated verification and manual parent-creation calls.</rationale>
</tool>
</rewrites>
</propose_rewrites>
